message:
fix: bellman_ford_path_length / dfs_*_nodes / immediate_dominators / dominance_frontiers unhashable parity (br-r37-c1-mz7g4)

Sixth follow-up to the unhashable-node series (br-r37-c1-m0io3 →
-g438p → -i9whv → -c4agn → -ybw1s). Five more node-parameter
functions raised the wrong exception class (or silently succeeded)
on unhashable / missing inputs:

  bellman_ford_path_length    fnx: NodeNotFound      nx: TypeError
  dfs_preorder_nodes          fnx: NetworkXError     nx: TypeError
  dfs_postorder_nodes         fnx: NetworkXError     nx: TypeError
  immediate_dominators        fnx: <silent ok>       nx: NetworkXError
  dominance_frontiers         fnx: <silent ok>       nx: NetworkXError

The first three need eager hash-validation up front (TypeError
matching nx).  ``immediate_dominators`` and ``dominance_frontiers``
need ``start in G`` membership pre-checks (silent-False on
unhashable, then NetworkXError) — fnx was silently delegating to
the Rust impl whereas nx pre-validates and raises NetworkXError.

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``:

- ``bellman_ford_path_length``: add ``hash(source)`` after the
  delegation gate, before the ``source not in G`` check (sibling
  fix to ``bellman_ford_path`` from br-r37-c1-c4agn).

- ``dfs_preorder_nodes`` / ``dfs_postorder_nodes``: restructure
  from inline-generator to outer-fn-returning-inner-``_gen()`` so
  ``hash(source)`` fires at call time, not first ``next()``.
  Matches the pattern already in ``dfs_tree`` / ``bfs_edges``.

- ``immediate_dominators``: pre-check ``start not in G`` (silent-
  False on unhashable, returns False) and raise
  ``NetworkXError("start is not in G")`` — matching nx's exact
  wording.

- ``dominance_frontiers``: was a Rust-direct re-export.  Wrap as
  a Python function that pre-checks ``start not in G`` and
  delegates to the renamed ``_raw_dominance_frontiers``.

``docs/coverage.md`` regenerated: ``dominance_frontiers`` moved
from RUST_NATIVE → PY_WRAPPER (RUST_NATIVE: 26 → 25, PY_WRAPPER:
850 → 851).

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_unhashable_followup_paths_2_parity.py``:
- 1 × bellman_ford_path_length parametrized over {list, set, dict}
- 2 × dfs_*_nodes parametrized × 3 unhashable types
- 2 × immediate_dominators / dominance_frontiers unhashable
  parametrized × 3
- 2 × hashable-but-missing start regression guards
- 5 × hashable-input regression guards (paths / dicts unchanged)

Full sweep
----------
6983 passed, 0 failed (was 6962 before — +21 new regression cases
+ pre-existing tests improved coverage).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-mnrem","title":"parity: fnx.approximation.* exposed (G, *args, **kwargs) catch-all signatures, hiding all real kwargs","description":"_ApproximationNamespace dynamically wrapped each approximation function as (G, *args, **kwargs), so inspect.signature(fnx.approximation.X) returned a useless catch-all for every function. nx exposes the documented per-function signatures (average_clustering(G, trials, seed), christofides(G, weight, tree), greedy_tsp(G, weight, source), etc). 27 functions affected. Drop-in callers couldn't see what kwargs are accepted via introspection, IDEs and type-checkers couldn't help, and typos passed silently into nx's **kwargs handling. Fix: functools.wraps(nx_func) on the wrapper so the original signature/name/docstring are preserved. 30 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:41:51.191871760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:42:37.315185389Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:42:37.313339806Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 63a0163. _ApproximationNamespace.__getattr__ now uses functools.wraps so every fnx.approximation.X carries nx's exact signature, name, and docstring. 30 parity tests freeze the contract; all 27 previously-mismatched functions now pass introspection.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-mpv5x","title":"parity: __str__ drift on view classes and Graph (name dropped, repr-style instead of bare-data)","description":"Several __str__ implementations diverge from nx: NodeView/EdgeView/DegreeView/NodeDataView/EdgeDataView/AtlasView/AdjacencyView fall back to __repr__ (e.g. 'NodeView((0, 1, 2))') instead of nx's bare-data form ('[0, 1, 2]'). Graph.__str__ ignores the graph name attribute: fnx returns 'Graph with 0 nodes and 0 edges' even when G.graph['name'] = 'G' is set; nx returns 'Graph named \'G\' with 0 nodes and 0 edges'. Drop-in code that does print(G), print(G.nodes), or asserts on str(view) gets different output. Fix: add explicit __str__ methods that return the bare-data forms matching nx exactly; fix Graph.__str__ to include the name when set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:36:59.610629652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:43:54.211977409Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:43:54.209976079Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 73c840f. View __str__ returns bare data (list/dict) matching nx; Graph.__str__ includes name when set. 15 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-msf5j","title":"DiGraph/Multi*.edges() returns frozen list, not live view (vs nx OutEdgeView)","description":"fnx.DiGraph.edges(), fnx.MultiGraph.edges(), and fnx.MultiDiGraph.edges() return a frozen list snapshot at call time. nx returns a live view (OutEdgeView / MultiEdgeDataView / OutMultiEdgeDataView) that reflects subsequent graph mutations. Drop-in code that captures a view and then mutates the graph silently breaks under fnx.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.DiGraph([(1,2),(2,3)])
  >>> v = g.edges()
  >>> g.add_edge(3, 4)
  >>> list(v)
  [(1, 2), (2, 3)]            # nx returns [(1,2),(2,3),(3,4)]

Root cause: _DiGraphEdgeView.__call__ at python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:808 always builds and returns a list. Same pattern in _MultiGraphEdgeView.__call__ (returns _EdgeListWithSetAlgebra, a list subclass). nx's EdgeView.__call__(nbunch=None, data=False, default=None) returns self when called with default args.

Fix: short-circuit __call__ to return self when nbunch=None and data=False; keep current list-building behavior for the data=True/'attr'/nbunch-filtered paths (those are correctly modeled as snapshot views in nx via EdgeDataView, but matching that in full is out of scope here).

Note: a parallel defect exists for MultiGraph.nodes() / MultiDiGraph.nodes() (Rust-side NodeView class) — separate bead.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T04:33:07.797286806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T04:40:27.272422580Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T04:40:27.269651843Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-mz7g4","title":"Unhashable parity: bellman_ford_path_length, dfs_*_nodes, immediate_dominators, dominance_frontiers","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T10:45:49.946659231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T10:46:18.749243188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-n7rgh","title":"7 directed-only fns raise wrong exception type on undirected input","description":"Audit of all 53 nx functions decorated with @not_implemented_for('undirected') found 7 functions that raise the wrong exception type on undirected input (Graph and/or MultiGraph):

  directed_edge_swap          fnx: NetworkXError ('requires a directed graph')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  in_degree_centrality(MG)    fnx: AttributeError ('no attr in_degree')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  out_degree_centrality(MG)   fnx: AttributeError ('no attr out_degree')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  moral_graph                 fnx: AttributeError ('no attr predecessors')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  reverse_view                fnx: NetworkXError ('Cannot reverse an undirected graph.')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  triad_type                  fnx: NetworkXError ('triad_type requires DiGraph')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  triadic_census              fnx: NetworkXError ('requires a directed graph')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented

Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXNotImplemented) doesn't trigger on fnx — neither NetworkXError nor AttributeError is a subclass of NetworkXNotImplemented.

Fix: add if not G.is_directed(): raise NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for undirected type') at the top of each wrapper, matching nx's @not_implemented_for('undirected') decorator.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T08:58:14.726580507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T09:07:49.374615914Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T09:07:49.371944103Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-nhz31","title":"test_coverage_gaps: refactor recent direct-nx wrappers + regenerate coverage.md","description":"Two pre-existing failures in test_coverage_gaps.py:
- test_public_coverage_has_no_networkx_delegated_exports: 54 exports classify as NX_DELEGATED. The test asserts == 0.
- test_generated_coverage_matrix_document_is_current: docs/coverage.md is out of date.

Most of the 54 NX_DELEGATED items pre-date my recent work. But my recent fixes added 6 more by directly importing nx in public functions:
- intersection (br-r37-c1-saf8a)
- k_truss (br-r37-c1-3qopf)
- maximum_spanning_tree (br-r37-c1-rtak4)
- minimum_spanning_tree (br-r37-c1-rtak4)
- transitive_closure_dag (br-r37-c1-utmy6)
- transitive_reduction (br-r37-c1-utmy6)

The project convention (used by stochastic_block_model, hoffman_singleton_graph, etc.) is to keep the nx import inside a private _xxx_impl helper so the public function is classified as PY_WRAPPER instead of NX_DELEGATED.

Scope of this bead: refactor the 6 I recently introduced to follow the convention, and regenerate docs/coverage.md. The other 48 pre-existing items are out of scope (separate refactor).

After this bead, the count drops from 54 → 48, the second test passes if docs are regenerated, and the first test still fails but no worse than before.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:53:43.443005498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:10.770550620Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:10.768479207Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ntkdn","title":"parity: random_sequence powerlaw/zipf/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence (4 fns)","description":"Four random-sequence utilities from networkx.utils.random_sequence not surfaced: powerlaw_sequence(n, exponent=2.0, seed=None), zipf_rv(alpha, xmin=1, seed=None), cumulative_distribution(distribution), is_valid_tree_degree_sequence(degree_sequence). Used by stochastic generators and degree-seq sanity checks. Add wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:04:43.303897526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.064336059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.062433818Z","close_reason":"Shipped in b08418c: powerlaw_sequence/zipf_rv/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence wrappers; all 4 verified against nx (n=20 exp=2.5 seed=42 powerlaw match exactly, cumulative_distribution = [0,0.5,0.8,1.0]).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -6256,35 +6256,57 @@ def dfs_successors(G, source=None, depth_limit=None, *, sort_neighbors=None):
 
 
 def dfs_preorder_nodes(G, source=None, depth_limit=None, *, sort_neighbors=None):
-    """Yield nodes in DFS preorder from source."""
-    try:
-        if sort_neighbors is not None:
-            for _, v, label in _py_dfs_labeled_edges(
-                G, source=source, depth_limit=depth_limit, sort_neighbors=sort_neighbors
-            ):
-                if label == "forward":
-                    yield v
-            return
-        nodes = _dfs_preorder_nodes_raw(G, source=source, depth_limit=depth_limit)
-    except NodeNotFound as exc:
-        raise NetworkXError(str(exc)) from exc
-    yield from nodes
+    """Yield nodes in DFS preorder from source.
+
+    br-r37-c1-mz7g4: hash-validate source eagerly (nx-shaped TypeError)
+    on unhashable inputs. Restructured as outer fn returning inner
+    generator so the hash check fires at call time, not first ``next()``.
+    """
+    if source is not None:
+        hash(source)
+
+    def _gen():
+        try:
+            if sort_neighbors is not None:
+                for _, v, label in _py_dfs_labeled_edges(
+                    G, source=source, depth_limit=depth_limit, sort_neighbors=sort_neighbors
+                ):
+                    if label == "forward":
+                        yield v
+                return
+            nodes = _dfs_preorder_nodes_raw(G, source=source, depth_limit=depth_limit)
+        except NodeNotFound as exc:
+            raise NetworkXError(str(exc)) from exc
+        yield from nodes
+
+    return _gen()
 
 
 def dfs_postorder_nodes(G, source=None, depth_limit=None, *, sort_neighbors=None):
-    """Yield nodes in DFS postorder from source."""
-    try:
-        if sort_neighbors is not None:
-            for _, v, label in _py_dfs_labeled_edges(
-                G, source=source, depth_limit=depth_limit, sort_neighbors=sort_neighbors
-            ):
-                if label == "reverse":
-                    yield v
-            return
-        nodes = _dfs_postorder_nodes_raw(G, source=source, depth_limit=depth_limit)
-    except NodeNotFound as exc:
-        raise NetworkXError(str(exc)) from exc
-    yield from nodes
+    """Yield nodes in DFS postorder from source.
+
+    br-r37-c1-mz7g4: hash-validate source eagerly (nx-shaped TypeError)
+    on unhashable inputs. Restructured as outer fn returning inner
+    generator so the hash check fires at call time, not first ``next()``.
+    """
+    if source is not None:
+        hash(source)
+
+    def _gen():
+        try:
+            if sort_neighbors is not None:
+                for _, v, label in _py_dfs_labeled_edges(
+                    G, source=source, depth_limit=depth_limit, sort_neighbors=sort_neighbors
+                ):
+                    if label == "reverse":
+                        yield v
+                return
+            nodes = _dfs_postorder_nodes_raw(G, source=source, depth_limit=depth_limit)
+        except NodeNotFound as exc:
+            raise NetworkXError(str(exc)) from exc
+        yield from nodes
+
+    return _gen()
 
 
 def dfs_tree(G, source=None, depth_limit=None, *, sort_neighbors=None):
@@ -9956,7 +9978,7 @@ def max_weight_clique(G, weight="weight"):
     is_aperiodic as _raw_is_aperiodic,
     antichains as _raw_antichains,
     immediate_dominators as _raw_immediate_dominators,
-    dominance_frontiers,

 )
 
 
@@ -9966,6 +9988,12 @@ def immediate_dominators(G, start):
     Matches upstream ``nx.immediate_dominators`` contract: the returned
     dict does **not** include ``start`` itself (franken_networkx-y87ra).
     """
+    # br-r37-c1-mz7g4: nx pre-validates ``start in G`` (silently
+    # returns False on unhashable, then raises NetworkXError);
+    # without this guard fnx silently computes whatever the Rust impl
+    # returns. Match nx's exact contract.
+    if start not in G:
+        raise NetworkXError("start is not in G")
     try:
         idom = _raw_immediate_dominators(G, start)
     except NetworkXNotImplemented as exc:
@@ -9982,6 +10010,18 @@ def immediate_dominators(G, start):
     return idom
 
 
+def dominance_frontiers(G, start):
+    """Dominance frontiers for every node reachable from ``start``.
+
+    br-r37-c1-mz7g4: nx pre-validates ``start in G`` (silently returns
+    False on unhashable, then raises NetworkXError); without this
+    guard fnx silently computes. Match nx's exact contract.
+    """
+    if start not in G:
+        raise NetworkXError("start is not in G")
+    return _raw_dominance_frontiers(G, start)
+
+
 def antichains(G, topo_order=None):
     """Generate all antichains in a DAG.
 
@@ -10172,6 +10212,9 @@ def bellman_ford_path_length(G, source, target, weight="weight"):
         return _call_networkx_for_parity(
             "bellman_ford_path_length", G, source, target, weight=weight
         )
+    # br-r37-c1-mz7g4: hash-validate source for nx-shaped TypeError
+    # parity on unhashable inputs (sibling fix to bellman_ford_path).
+    hash(source)
     if source not in G:
         raise NodeNotFound(f"Source {source} not in G")
     if target not in G:

@@ -0,0 +1,173 @@
+"""Parity for bellman_ford_path_length, dfs_*_nodes, immediate_dominators,
+dominance_frontiers on unhashable / missing inputs.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-mz7g4 (sixth follow-up to the unhashable-node series:
+br-r37-c1-m0io3 → -g438p → -i9whv → -c4agn → -ybw1s).
+
+Five drifts found in the post-ybw1s probe:
+
+  bellman_ford_path_length    fnx: NodeNotFound       nx: TypeError
+  dfs_preorder_nodes          fnx: NetworkXError      nx: TypeError
+  dfs_postorder_nodes         fnx: NetworkXError      nx: TypeError
+  immediate_dominators        fnx: <silent ok>        nx: NetworkXError
+  dominance_frontiers         fnx: <silent ok>        nx: NetworkXError
+
+The first three need eager hash-validation (TypeError on unhashable to
+match nx).  ``immediate_dominators`` and ``dominance_frontiers`` need
+``start in G`` membership pre-checks (silent-False on unhashable, then
+NetworkXError) — fnx was silently computing whereas nx pre-validates.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+UNHASHABLE = [
+    pytest.param([1, 2], id="list"),
+    pytest.param({1, 2}, id="set"),
+    pytest.param({"a": 1}, id="dict"),
+]
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# bellman_ford_path_length — TypeError on unhashable source
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+def test_bellman_ford_path_length_unhashable_source_raises_typeerror(val):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        fnx.bellman_ford_path_length(G, val, 1)
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        nx.bellman_ford_path_length(GX, val, 1)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# dfs_preorder_nodes / dfs_postorder_nodes — TypeError on unhashable source
+# (eager — fires at call time, not at first ``next()``)
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+def test_dfs_preorder_nodes_unhashable_source_raises_typeerror(val):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        fnx.dfs_preorder_nodes(G, val)
+    # nx is lazy — only raises on first next()
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        list(nx.dfs_preorder_nodes(GX, val))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+def test_dfs_postorder_nodes_unhashable_source_raises_typeerror(val):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        fnx.dfs_postorder_nodes(G, val)
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        list(nx.dfs_postorder_nodes(GX, val))
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# immediate_dominators / dominance_frontiers — NetworkXError on unhashable/missing
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+def test_immediate_dominators_unhashable_start_raises_networkxerror(val):
+    """nx pre-checks ``start in G`` (silent-False on unhashable) and
+    raises NetworkXError. fnx was silently computing whatever the
+    Rust impl produced — now matches."""
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"start is not in G"):
+        fnx.immediate_dominators(G, val)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"start is not in G"):
+        nx.immediate_dominators(GX, val)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+def test_dominance_frontiers_unhashable_start_raises_networkxerror(val):
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"start is not in G"):
+        fnx.dominance_frontiers(G, val)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"start is not in G"):
+        nx.dominance_frontiers(GX, val)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_immediate_dominators_missing_hashable_start_raises_networkxerror():
+    """Hashable but absent start node — same NetworkXError contract."""
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"start is not in G"):
+        fnx.immediate_dominators(G, 99)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"start is not in G"):
+        nx.immediate_dominators(GX, 99)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_dominance_frontiers_missing_hashable_start_raises_networkxerror():
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"start is not in G"):
+        fnx.dominance_frontiers(G, 99)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"start is not in G"):
+        nx.dominance_frontiers(GX, 99)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Regression — hashable inputs unaffected
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_bellman_ford_path_length_hashable_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2, {"weight": 3.0}), (2, 3, {"weight": 4.0})])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2, {"weight": 3.0}), (2, 3, {"weight": 4.0})])
+    assert fnx.bellman_ford_path_length(G, 1, 3) == nx.bellman_ford_path_length(GX, 1, 3)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_dfs_preorder_nodes_hashable_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
+    assert list(fnx.dfs_preorder_nodes(G, 1)) == list(nx.dfs_preorder_nodes(GX, 1))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_dfs_postorder_nodes_hashable_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
+    assert list(fnx.dfs_postorder_nodes(G, 1)) == list(nx.dfs_postorder_nodes(GX, 1))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_immediate_dominators_hashable_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (1, 3)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (1, 3)])
+    assert dict(fnx.immediate_dominators(G, 1)) == dict(nx.immediate_dominators(GX, 1))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_dominance_frontiers_hashable_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (1, 3)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (1, 3)])
+    f = fnx.dominance_frontiers(G, 1)
+    n = nx.dominance_frontiers(GX, 1)
+    assert {k: set(v) for k, v in f.items()} == {k: set(v) for k, v in n.items()}
